([Dhivehi](/wiki/Dhivehi_language "Dhivehi language"): ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭ ގައުމީ ފުޓްބޯލް ޓީމް) represents [the Maldives](/wiki/The_Maldives "The Maldives") in international [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and is controlled by the [Football Association of Maldives](/wiki/Football_Association_of_Maldives "Football Association of Maldives"). It is a member of the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC).

The **Maldives national football team** ([Dhivehi](/wiki/Dhivehi_language "Dhivehi language"): ދިވެހިރާއްޖޭ ގައުމީ ފުޓްބޯލް ޓީމް) represents [the Maldives](/wiki/The_Maldives "The Maldives") in international [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and is controlled by the [Football Association of Maldives](/wiki/Football_Association_of_Maldives "Football Association of Maldives"). It is a member of the [Asian Football Confederation](/wiki/Asian_Football_Confederation "Asian Football Confederation") (AFC).

The Maldives' most significant success was winning the [2008 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2008_SAFF_Championship "2008 SAFF Championship") where they beat the most successful team [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team") in the final 1–0.[[4]](#cite_note-4) They won their second local title in [2018 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2018_SAFF_Championship "2018 SAFF Championship"), once again by beating India in the final.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### South Asian Football Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: South Asian Football Championship")]

#### 1997 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1997 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup")]

See also: [1997 SAFF Gold Cup](/wiki/1997_SAFF_Gold_Cup "1997 SAFF Gold Cup")

This was the third edition of SAFF Cup which was held in [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal"), and it was Maldives' first campaign in this regional tournament.

Maldives was placed in the [group B](/wiki/1997_SAFF_Gold_Cup#Group_B "1997 SAFF Gold Cup") with [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team") and [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh_national_football_team "Bangladesh national football team"), where they advanced into the semi-final as the group's second team with two points in hand. They drew their first match against Bangladesh with the help of Lirugham Saeed's only goal. In the second match, they drew against the India 2–2, in which Ibrahim Rasheed and [Mohamed Nizam](/wiki/Mohamed_Nizam "Mohamed Nizam") came scoring when the Red Snappers were at a two-goal deposit.

Maldives went on to win the semi-final match 2–1 against [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team "Sri Lanka national football team") with [Shah Ismail](/wiki/Shah_Ismail "Shah Ismail") and Mohamed Nizam on the scoreline, but lost the final match to India by 5–1, where Adam Abdul Latheef scored the only goal for Maldives.

#### 1999 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 1999 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup")]

See also: [1999 SAFF Gold Cup](/wiki/1999_SAFF_Gold_Cup "1999 SAFF Gold Cup")

In this edition of SAFF Cup, hosted in [Fatorda Stadium](/wiki/Fatorda_Stadium "Fatorda Stadium"), [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa"), [India](/wiki/India "India"), Maldives were grouped with [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_national_football_team "Nepal national football team") and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team "Sri Lanka national football team"), where Maldives finished at the top of the [group B](/wiki/1999_SAFF_Gold_Cup#Group_B "1999 SAFF Gold Cup").

First match against Sri Lanka ended as a goalless draw while they managed to win 3–2 against the Nepal. [Shah Ismail](/wiki/Shah_Ismail "Shah Ismail"), Mohamed Wildhan and [Mausoom Abdul Gafoor](/wiki/Mausoom_Abdul_Gafoor "Mausoom Abdul Gafoor") scored the goals for Maldives in this match.

Maldives had to face the two times SAFF Cup champion India in the semi-final and lost the game by 2–1, where Mohamed Wildhan scored against the [Blue Tigers](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team"). Though they failed to qualify for the Final of the tournament, they claimed the bronze by defeating Nepal in the third place play-off match by 2–0. Mohamed Wildhan and Mohamed Ibrahim were the goal scorers.

Mohamed Wildhan shared the top scorer award with Nepal's Naresh Joshi, India's Baichung Bhutia and Bangladesh's Mihazur Rehman scoring three goals each in this competition.

#### 2003 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2003 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup")]

See also: [2003 SAFF Gold Cup](/wiki/2003_SAFF_Gold_Cup "2003 SAFF Gold Cup")

In the 2003 SAFF Gold Cup held in [Dhaka](/wiki/Dhaka "Dhaka"), [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh "Bangladesh"), Maldives were drawn in the [group B](/wiki/2003_SAFF_Gold_Cup#Group_B "2003 SAFF Gold Cup") along with [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh_national_football_team "Bangladesh national football team"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_national_football_team "Nepal national football team") and [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan_national_football_team "Bhutan national football team").

Maldives won their first match 6–0 a record win of the highest number of goals scored by a team in a single match in this tournament history against Bhutan, with Maldives' first hat-trick in the regional tournament, scored by [Ali Shiham](/wiki/Ali_Shiham "Ali Shiham"). [Mohamed Nizam](/wiki/Mohamed_Nizam "Mohamed Nizam"), [Ashraf Luthfy](/wiki/Ashraf_Luthfy "Ashraf Luthfy") and [Ali Umar](/wiki/Ali_Umar "Ali Umar") scored one goal each in this victory. Despite the 1–0 loss to Bangladesh in the second match, Maldives won their third match against Nepal and they were through into the semi-final of the tournament as the group runners-up. Mohamed Nizam, Ashraf Luthfy and Ali Umar scored in their 3–2 win over Nepal.

[Ibrahim Fazeel](/wiki/Ibrahim_Fazeel "Ibrahim Fazeel") scored the only goal against [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_national_football_team "Pakistan national football team") in the semi-final, which helped them to reach the final for the second time. Ali Umar's only goal in the second half helped Maldives to survive until the penalty shootout in the final match against Bangladesh, but Maldives lost 5–3 at the [Bangabandhu National Stadium](/wiki/Bangabandhu_National_Stadium "Bangabandhu National Stadium") where Ahmed Naaz, Ismail Naseem and Ibrahim Fazeel succeeded to convert it from the spot but Ashraf Luthfy failed.

#### 2005 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2005 South Asian Football Federation Gold Cup")]

See also: [2005 SAFF Gold Cup](/wiki/2005_SAFF_Gold_Cup "2005 SAFF Gold Cup")

In 2005, the tournament was held in [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") and Maldives were drawn with [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan_national_football_team "Afghanistan national football team") and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team "Sri Lanka national football team") along with the host [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_national_football_team "Pakistan national football team") in [group A](/wiki/2005_SAFF_Gold_Cup#Group_A "2005 SAFF Gold Cup").

Maldives started their campaign by breaking their own record created in the previous edition [2003](/wiki/2003_SAFF_Gold_Cup "2003 SAFF Gold Cup"), scoring a highest number of goals by a team in a single match. They won the first match 9–1 against Afghanistan. [Ibrahim Fazeel](/wiki/Ibrahim_Fazeel "Ibrahim Fazeel") and [Ahmed Thoriq](/wiki/Ahmed_Thoriq "Ahmed Thoriq") both scored a hat-trick while [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq") scored a brace and [Ali Umar](/wiki/Ali_Umar "Ali Umar") scored their opener. They won their second match against Sri Lanka 2–0 with goals from Ali Ashfaq and Ali Umar, while they ended at the top of the group, leaving the host Pakistan in second even after the goalless draw against them in the final group stage match.

Even though Maldives lost the semi-final match against India by one goal to nil, the top scorer award was shared by three of the Maldivian players; Ibrahim hassan, Ali Ashfaq and Ahmed Thoriq scored three goals each.

[2008 SAFF Championship Final](/wiki/2008_SAFF_Championship#Final "2008 SAFF Championship") starting lineup on June 14, 2008, in [Sugathadasa Stadium](/wiki/Sugathadasa_Stadium "Sugathadasa Stadium") ([Colombo](/wiki/Colombo "Colombo")).

See also: [2008 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2008_SAFF_Championship "2008 SAFF Championship")

The 2008 SAFF Championship draw, which took place on 26 February 2007, placed Maldives in [group A](/wiki/2008_SAFF_Championship#Group_A "2008 SAFF Championship"), alongside [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_national_football_team "Nepal national football team"), [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team"), and [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_national_football_team "Pakistan national football team"). Maldives started with a comfortable 3–0 win over Pakistan, with the goals from [Mohamed Shifan](/wiki/Mohamed_Shifan "Mohamed Shifan"), [Ahmed Thoriq](/wiki/Ahmed_Thoriq "Ahmed Thoriq") and an own goal from [Naveed Akram](/wiki/Naveed_Akram "Naveed Akram").[[5]](#cite_note-5) In their second match they defeated Nepal 4–1, with two goals apiece from [Ismail Mohamed](/wiki/Ismail_Mohamed_(football_player) "Ismail Mohamed (football player)") and [Ibrahim Fazeel](/wiki/Ibrahim_Fazeel "Ibrahim Fazeel").[[6]](#cite_note-6) They lost their last group stage match against [India](/wiki/India_national_football_team "India national football team"), 0–1.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

They advanced to the semi-finals and defeated [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team "Sri Lanka national football team") 0–1, with a brilliant goal by [Ibrahim Fazeel](/wiki/Ibrahim_Fazeel "Ibrahim Fazeel") on a pass from [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq"). They then advanced to the final for the third time in [SAFF Championship](/wiki/SAFF_Championship "SAFF Championship") history.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In the final three minutes of full-time during the SAFF Championship final against the four time SAFF Championship Champions [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team"), [Mukhthar Naseer](/wiki/Mukhthar_Naseer "Mukhthar Naseer") scored a single goal from a corner kick taken by [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq"), winning the SAFF Championship for Maldives for the first time in their history. Maldives won the SAFF Championship by scoring nine goals and conceding two.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 2008 SAFF Championship, [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq") won the Best Player award. Maldives was the team who scored the most goals in this year's competition along with their rivals India. They were also the team who conceded the fewest goals in the tournament.

The SAFF Championship was Maldives first ever gold medal in Maldivian Football history, and to celebrate this memorable day the President declared that 15 June 2008 will be a public holiday. The only goal scored in the final match, by Mukhthar, was his first international goal for the Maldives national football team.

#### 2009 SAFF Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2009 SAFF Championship")]

See also: [2009 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2009_SAFF_Championship "2009 SAFF Championship")

Maldives were drawn with the rivals India in [group A](/wiki/2009_SAFF_Championship#Draw "2009 SAFF Championship"), along with [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan_national_football_team "Afghanistan national football team") and Nepal.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Maldives started their campaign as the defending champions, facing a 1–1 draw where [The Gorkhalis](/wiki/Nepal_national_football_team "Nepal national football team") did not allow the red snappers win the match with the only goal by [Ahmed Thoriq](/wiki/Ahmed_Thoriq "Ahmed Thoriq"). In their second match they defeated Afghanistan by 3–1 with two goals from [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq") and one from Ahmed Thoriq. Their next match against rival India on 9 December was won by 2–0, Ahmed Thoriq and [Ibrahim Fazeel](/wiki/Ibrahim_Fazeel "Ibrahim Fazeel") scoring the goals which took them into the semi-finals as group A winners, finishing the group stage ahead of India.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

In the semi-final, they defeated Sri Lanka with a comfortable score line of 5–1[[12]](#cite_note-12) where Ibrahim Fazeel scored two goals and Ahmed Thoriq, Ali Ashfaq and [Ashad Ali](/wiki/Ashad_Ali "Ashad Ali") scored one goal each, but they lost 3–1 to eventual winners India from a penalty shootout in the final after playing 120 minutes without seeing a goal from either sides. Ibrahim Fazeel was the only player to score in the penalty shootout for Maldives while Ahmed Thoriq, [Mukhthar Naseer](/wiki/Mukhthar_Naseer "Mukhthar Naseer") and Ali Ashfaq failed to convert it from the spot.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

Ahmed Thoriq shared the top scorer award with Bangladesh's [Enamul Haque](/wiki/Enamul_Haque_(footballer) "Enamul Haque (footballer)") and Sri Lanka's [Channa Ediri Bandanage](/wiki/Channa_Ediri_Bandanage "Channa Ediri Bandanage") scoring four goals each in this competition.

This was the second time Maldives lost the [SAFF Championship](/wiki/SAFF_Championship "SAFF Championship") final in penalties, and [Bangabandhu National Stadium](/wiki/Bangabandhu_National_Stadium "Bangabandhu National Stadium") was the venue on both occasions.

#### 2011 SAFF Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: 2011 SAFF Championship")]

See also: [2011 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2011_SAFF_Championship "2011 SAFF Championship")

The 2011 SAFF Championship hosted in [Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, Delhi](/wiki/Jawaharlal_Nehru_Stadium,_Delhi "Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium, Delhi") was the tournament with Maldives' worst result. Even though Maldives managed to qualify for the semi-finals as the group winner, Maldives ended their campaign with only one win, two draws and losing one game, scoring and conceding 5 goals.

Maldives were in the group B, drawn with [Bangladesh](/wiki/Bangladesh_national_football_team "Bangladesh national football team"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal_national_football_team "Nepal national football team") and [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_national_football_team "Pakistan national football team"). They drew 1–1 against Nepal with [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq")'s only goal and their second game against Pakistan ended goalless. Maldives finished the group stage with a 3–1 win over Bangladesh, with [Ahmed Thoriq](/wiki/Ahmed_Thoriq "Ahmed Thoriq")'s brace and Ali Ashfaq's goal.

Maldives lost 3–1 to [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team") in the semi-final, [Shamweel Qasim](/wiki/Shamweel_Qasim "Shamweel Qasim") scoring Maldives' only goal.

#### 2013 SAFF Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: 2013 SAFF Championship")]

See also: [2013 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2013_SAFF_Championship "2013 SAFF Championship")

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4a/SAFF_Championship_2013_%2811%29.JPG/250px-SAFF_Championship_2013_%2811%29.JPG)](/wiki/File:SAFF_Championship_2013_(11).JPG)

Maldives at the right against [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan_national_football_team "Bhutan national football team") at the [2013 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2013_SAFF_Championship "2013 SAFF Championship")

Maldives were drawn in the group B alongside [Afghanistan](/wiki/Afghanistan_national_football_team "Afghanistan national football team"), [Bhutan](/wiki/Bhutan_national_football_team "Bhutan national football team") and [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka_national_football_team "Sri Lanka national football team").

They started the group stage by renewing their record of scoring the most goals by a team in a single game; winning 10–0 against Sri Lanka.[[14]](#cite_note-14) In this match, skipper [Ali Ashfaq](/wiki/Ali_Ashfaq "Ali Ashfaq") scored a double hat-trick and broke the record of India's [IM Vijayan](/wiki/IM_Vijayan "IM Vijayan") who holds the record of all-time top scorer of the tournament with 12 goals.[[15]](#cite_note-15) He also made a new record of scoring the highest number of goals by a single player in a match in the tournament. Remaining goals of this match came from [Assadhulla Abdulla](/wiki/Assadhulla_Abdulla "Assadhulla Abdulla"), [Hassan Adhuham](/wiki/Hassan_Adhuham "Hassan Adhuham"), [Ali Fasir](/wiki/Ali_Fasir "Ali Fasir") and [Ali Umar](/wiki/Ali_Umar "Ali Umar").

In their second match, Ali Ashfaq scored four goals and Ali Fasir scored a brace while [Mohammad Umair](/wiki/Mohammad_Umair "Mohammad Umair") and Ali Umar scored one goal each in the 8–2 win against Bhutan.[[16]](#cite_note-16) Maldives' third match of the group stage against Afghanistan ended goalless.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

However, Maldives faced [India](/wiki/India_men%27s_national_football_team "India men's national football team") for a record fourth time in a semi final match of this competition, and never won a semi final match against them as Maldives lost 1–0 in this semi final match at the [Dasarath Rangasala Stadium](/wiki/Dasarath_Rangasala_Stadium "Dasarath Rangasala Stadium"), [Kathmandu](/wiki/Kathmandu "Kathmandu"), [Nepal](/wiki/Nepal "Nepal").[[18]](#cite_note-18)

Ali Ashfaq won the Golden Boot Award, scoring 10 goals in the competition.[[19]](#cite_note-19) He now holds the record of competition's all-time top scorer with 18 goals, and the player to score the highest number of goals in one SAFF Championship; 10. India's IM Vijayan was the previous player to hold this record, scoring 6 goals in the [1997](/wiki/1997_SAFF_Gold_Cup "1997 SAFF Gold Cup") edition.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8c/Maldives_National_Football_Team_in_the_2018_SAFF_Championship_final.jpg/250px-Maldives_National_Football_Team_in_the_2018_SAFF_Championship_final.jpg)](/wiki/File:Maldives_National_Football_Team_in_the_2018_SAFF_Championship_final.jpg)

[2018 SAFF Championship final](/wiki/2018_SAFF_Championship_final "2018 SAFF Championship final") starting lineup on 15 September 2018 at [Bangabandhu National Stadium](/wiki/Bangabandhu_National_Stadium "Bangabandhu National Stadium"), [Dhaka](/wiki/Dhaka "Dhaka"), Bangladesh.

Maldives was drawn in a group with Sri Lanka and India, where they drew with Sri Lanka at 0–0 and lost to India 2–0. Due to Maldives and Sri Lanka having accumulated the same points, a coin toss was held to decide which team will advance to the semi-finals against Nepal. Luck was in favor of Maldives as they won the coin toss and the match against Nepal 3–0.

This meant that Maldives would once against compete with India in the finals. During the first half of the match Maldives player [Ibrahim Mahudhee](/wiki/Ibrahim_Mahudhee "Ibrahim Mahudhee") scored the opening goal and in the second half [Hamza Mohamed](/wiki/Hamza_Mohamed "Hamza Mohamed") makes an excellent threaded through pass that allowed [Ali Fasir](/wiki/Ali_Fasir "Ali Fasir") to score the second goal for Maldives. India's [Sumeet Passi](/wiki/Sumeet_Passi "Sumeet Passi") scored a consolation goal for India in extra time.

The match ended with Maldives as the victors of the 2018 SAFF Suzuki championship making them the second team in the competition to have ever won the Cup more than once. Maldives goal keeper [Mohamed Faisal](/wiki/Mohamed_Faisal_(footballer) "Mohamed Faisal (footballer)") won the MVP award of the competition for his work between the sticks.

### Asian Cup and World Cup qualifications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Asian Cup and World Cup qualifications")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/73/MV_National_Team_2015.jpg/250px-MV_National_Team_2015.jpg)](/wiki/File:MV_National_Team_2015.jpg)

President [Abdulla Yameen](/wiki/Abdulla_Yameen "Abdulla Yameen") attending the training session of Maldives national team, ahead of their World Cup qualifying match against Qatar.

Maldives has never qualified for such big tournaments and often classified as a weaker team in the continent. Nonetheless, Maldives achieved several impressive results. During [2006 World Cup qualification](/wiki/2006_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(AFC) "2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)"), Maldives stunned by drawing [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea_national_football_team "South Korea national football team"), an Asian powerhouse, 0–0 at home; and defeating [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam_national_football_team "Vietnam national football team"), a rising Asian team, in the same ground with an impressive 3–0. However, its worst defeat also occurred in the World Cup qualification as they were thrashed 0–17 by [Iran](/wiki/Iran_national_football_team "Iran national football team") in [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus") during [1998 World Cup qualification](/wiki/1998_FIFA_World_Cup_qualification_(AFC) "1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)").

### Home stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Home stadium")]

[National Football Stadium](/wiki/National_Football_Stadium_(Maldives) "National Football Stadium (Maldives)") ([Dhivehi](/wiki/Dhivehi_language "Dhivehi language"): ގައުމީ ފުޓުބޯޅަ ދަނޑު) is a [multi-purpose stadium](/wiki/Multi-purpose_stadium "Multi-purpose stadium") in [Malé](/wiki/Mal%C3%A9 "Malé"), [Maldives](/wiki/Maldives "Maldives"). It is used mostly for [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") matches of the [Dhivehi League](/wiki/Dhivehi_League "Dhivehi League"), [Maldives FA Cup](/wiki/Maldives_FA_Cup "Maldives FA Cup"), and International matches. The stadium holds around 11,850 spectators.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

### Supporters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Maldives_national_football_team&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Supporters")]

About 500,000 Maldivian supporters, including the former president [Maumoon Abdul Gayyoom](/wiki/Maumoon_Abdul_Gayoom "Maumoon Abdul Gayoom"), attended the [2008 SAFF Championship](/wiki/2008_SAFF_Championship "2008 SAFF Championship") final match in [Sugathadasa Stadium](/wiki/Sugathadasa_Stadium "Sugathadasa Stadium"), [Colombo](/wiki/Colombo "Colombo"), [Sri Lanka](/wiki/Sri_Lanka "Sri Lanka"). After their win, supporters together with ministers, gave a warm welcome to the team. To celebrate this memorable day,;the president declared that 15 June 2008 will be a public holiday.
